sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: {"pathway_id": "scc_locoregional_medically_fit", "title": "Squamous Cell Carcinoma - Locoregional Disease (Stage I-IVA, except T4b/unresectable N3) - Medically Fit for Surgery", "branching_basis": ["Histology (SCC)", "Clinical stage (T and N categories)", "Tumor location (proximal, middle, distal)", "Performance status", "Patient preference for surgery"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "pTis (in situ carcinoma) or pT1a (lamina propria/muscularis mucosae invasion).", "options": [{"type": "Endoscopic therapy", "label": "Endoscopic resection (ER) ± ablation", "preference": "Preferred", "recommendation": "ER (EMR or ESD) ± ablation is the preferred primary treatment for patients with pTis or pT1a disease. Ablation may be needed if there is multifocal high-grade dysplasia/carcinoma in situ. The goal is complete removal/eradication of early-stage disease and pre-neoplastic tissue.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "Surgical", "label": "Esophagectomy", "preference": "Alternative", "recommendation": "Esophagectomy is indicated for patients with extensive pTis or pT1a, especially nodular disease not adequately controlled by endoscopic therapy.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "N/A. After successful endoscopic therapy, patients undergo endoscopic surveillance per ESOPH-A. After esophagectomy, surveillance per ESOPH-9."}}, {"criteria": "pT1b, N0 (submucosal invasion, no lymph node metastasis).", "options": [{"type": "Surgical", "label": "Esophagectomy", "preference": "Preferred", "recommendation": "Esophagectomy (transhiatal, transthoracic, or minimally invasive; gastric reconstruction preferred) is the recommended primary treatment. A feeding jejunostomy may be placed for postoperative nutritional support.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "N/A. Surveillance per ESOPH-9."}}, {"criteria": "cT1b-cT2,N0 (low-risk lesions: <3 cm, well-differentiated).", "options": [{"type": "Surgical", "label": "Esophagectomy", "preference": "Preferred", "recommendation": "Esophagectomy is the recommended primary treatment for low-risk cT1b-cT2,N0 lesions.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "N/A. Surveillance per ESOPH-9."}}, {"criteria": "cT2,N0 (high-risk lesions: LVI, ≥3 cm, poorly differentiated) OR cT1b-cT2,N+ OR cT3-cT4a, Any N.", "options": [{"type": "Neoadjuvant/Perioperative", "label": "Preoperative chemoradiation", "preference": "Preferred (for cT2,N0 high-risk and cT1b-cT2,N+)", "recommendation": "Preoperative chemoradiation is the preferred primary treatment. Preferred regimens include paclitaxel and carboplatin (category 1) or fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (category 1). Histologic confirmation of suspected positive nodes is desirable.", "linked_regimen_ids": ["scc_preop_crt_taxol_carbo", "scc_preop_crt_folfox"]}, {"type": "Definitive", "label": "Definitive chemoradiation", "preference": "Preferred (for cT3-cT4a)", "recommendation": "Definitive chemoradiation is appropriate for patients who decline surgery or have cT4b (unresectable) tumors. Preferred regimens are the same as for preoperative chemoradiation.", "linked_regimen_ids": ["scc_definitive_crt_taxol_carbo", "scc_definitive_crt_folfox"]}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "After preoperative chemoradiation, restaging with FDG-PET/CT and EGD/biopsy is performed. Management based on response (see treatment_response_assessment). After definitive chemoradiation, surveillance per ESOPH-9."}}, {"criteria": "cT4b (unresectable: invasion of trachea, great vessels, vertebral body, or heart).", "options": [{"type": "Definitive", "label": "Chemotherapy alone", "preference": "Consider", "recommendation": "Consider chemotherapy alone in the setting of invasion of trachea, great vessels, or heart.", "linked_regimen_ids": ["scc_systemic_chemo"]}, {"type": "Palliative", "label": "Palliative/endoluminal stenting", "preference": "Consider", "recommendation": "For select patients, consider endoluminal stenting when appropriate for symptom palliation.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "These patients are generally managed with palliative intent (ESOPH-10)."}}]}

sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: {"pathway_id": "scc_locoregional_nonsurgical", "title": "Squamous Cell Carcinoma - Locoregional Disease (Stage I-IVA, except T4b/unresectable N3) - Nonsurgical Candidates", "branching_basis": ["Histology (SCC)", "Clinical stage", "Performance status (ability to tolerate chemoradiation)", "Patient preference"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "pTis, pT1a (nonsurgical candidate).", "options": [{"type": "Endoscopic therapy", "label": "ER ± ablation or ablation alone", "preference": "Preferred", "recommendation": "Endoscopic therapies (ER ± ablation, or ablation for pTis) are the recommended primary treatment for nonsurgical candidates with pTis or pT1a disease.", "linked_regimen_ids": []}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "Follow-up with endo"}}]}

flowcharts.title: Squamous Cell Carcinoma - Locoregional Disease (Stage I-IVA, except T4b/unresectable N3) - Medically Fit for Surgery

nodes.label: pTis (in situ carcinoma) or pT1a (lamina propria/muscularis mucosae invasion).
pT1b, N0 (submucosal invasion, no lymph node metastasis).
cT2,N0 (high-risk lesions: LVI, ≥3 cm, poorly differentiated) OR cT1b-cT2,N+ OR cT3-cT4a, Any N.
cT4b (unresectable: invasion of trachea, great vessels, vertebral body, or heart).
Esophagectomy (transhiatal, transthoracic, or minimally invasive; gastric reconstruction preferred)
Preoperative chemoradiation (paclitaxel and carboplatin or fluorouracil and oxaliplatin)
Definitive chemoradiation (paclitaxel and carboplatin or fluorouracil and oxaliplatin)

edges.label: Preferred primary treatment for pTis/pT1a. Endoscopic resection (ER) ± ablation.
Alternative for extensive pTis/pT1a, especially nodular disease not controlled by endoscopic therapy.
Recommended primary treatment for low-risk cT1b-cT2,N0.
Preferred for cT2,N0 high-risk and cT1b-cT2,N+ (category 1).
Preferred for cT3-cT4a, Any N, or for patients who decline surgery.
Consider in setting of invasion of trachea, great vessels, or heart.
Consider for select patients for symptom palliation.

flowcharts.title: Squamous Cell Carcinoma - Locoregional Disease (Stage I-IVA, except T4b/unresectable N3) - Nonsurgical Candidates

edges.label: Preferred primary treatment for nonsurgical candidates with pTis or pT1a.
